assessment_items.question_content:
**In the medieval Catholic belief system, people who committed sins could atone for them with good works, such as paying to have a chapel decorated with a religious narrative.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Paying for religious art was considered a good deed in the medieval West.**

assessment_items.question_content: **The narrative scenes in the Arena Chapel begin with [[☃ dropdown 1]], move on to [[☃ dropdown 2]], and end with scenes of the [[☃ dropdown 3]].**
hints: **The narrative scenes in the Arena Chapel are in three registers. The story spirals around the chapel starting at the top and ending at the bottom. The story moves linearly through the lives of Mary and Christ.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the scene of the Last Judgment, the damned in Hell suffer according to the whims of the devil and his demons.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/88831b6208fc409526e247a0fb02c9bb.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **In medieval scenes of the Last Judgment, and here in the Arena Chapel, the damned in Hell suffer according to their sins.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**[[☃ dropdown 1]] sits in the center of the Last Judgment with the elect on his right and the damned on his left.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a27a6a8aa6c7173fe9bd5e380bc7d41.jpg)
hints: **In scenes of the Last Judgment, Christ is at the center directing the action.**

assessment_items.question_content: **Giotto was probably [[☃ dropdown 1]]'s student and learned chiaroscuro, or the use of light and shadow, from him.**
hints: **It is likely that Giotto learned about chiaroscuro from Cimabue, another  Florentine artist who was interested in creating realistic forms in painting.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Arrest of Christ/Kiss of Judas shows . . .**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7ca9545c340980d83ca3267112fc4263.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The narrative scenes in the Arena Chapel were innovative for their use of intense drama, which brought the scenes to life in the minds of viewers.**

choices.content: the flatness of the picture plane.
Giotto's interest in patterns and stylization.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Lamentation shows Mary holding the dead Christ after the Crucifixion. This scene recalls . . .**

![Fresco showing mourners surrounding the body of Christ with halos and angels above, expressing grief in a vivid blue sky.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/60ef854bf5142a739cfefb7d2afd65a2.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Mary at the Lamentation recalls Mary at the Nativity (the story of the birth of Christ).**

choices.content: ![Fresco of the Nativity showing the infant Christ in swaddling clothes with halos, attended by Mary.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15c1982041a3ab26e2359328b6029463.jpg)
![Fresco showing two haloed figures, Joachim and Anne, embracing and kissing.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/edc772250234335e62bde612b0311b8b.jpg)
![Fresco showing Simeon holding the infant Christ, both with golden halos, during the Presentation at the Temple.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5dc97093e0d5cc9c9faf8501a38878e7.jpg)
![Fresco of the Virgin Mary holding the infant Christ, both with golden halos.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abb0db8736b3fe37d4a85bbfa9eb5ce6.jpg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Mary shows her [[☃ dropdown 1]] in the extreme grief in her facial expression.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5eb75b51513c0fe4fdf55e7db41fef78.jpg)
hints: **Giotto emphasizes Mary and Christ's humanity in this scene.**
